# The Secret Garden

Frances Hodgson Burnett's novel is well suited for observing setting, transformation, friendship, and the relationship between outward habits and inward life.

## Spelling Words

| Core Words | Challenge Words |
|---|---|
| flea | transformation |
| robin | disagreeable |
| key | curiosity |
| manor | loneliness |
| moor | friendship |
| plant | restoration |
| seed | cheerful |
| earth | stubbornness |
| bloom | invalid |
| fresh | perseverance |

## Word Study

- Sort words into nature words and character words.
- Track words that show growth or change.
- Write five sensory phrases about a garden: sight, sound, smell, touch, and movement.

## Narration

- Retell the discovery of the garden as a sequence of clues.
- Explain how Mary's habits change through the story.
- Describe how the garden affects more than one character.

## Writing Prompts

- Write a before-and-after description of a neglected place restored to beauty.
- Explain why secrets can be harmful, helpful, or both.
- Compare physical health and cheerfulness in the novel.

::: {.discussion-box}
**Open your response packet**

- [Emma - The Secret Garden](https://docs.google.com/document/d/EMMA_SECRET_GARDEN_GOOGLE_DOC_ID/edit)
- [James - The Secret Garden](https://docs.google.com/document/d/JAMES_SECRET_GARDEN_GOOGLE_DOC_ID/edit)

Write under the next open assignment heading in your book packet.
:::

## Projects

- Design a garden plan with labels for paths, plants, and hidden places.
- Keep a one-week nature journal with sketches and observations.
- Make a transformation timeline for Mary, Colin, and the garden.

::: {.discussion-box}
**Open your project report packet**

- [Emma - The Secret Garden](https://docs.google.com/document/d/EMMA_SECRET_GARDEN_GOOGLE_DOC_ID/edit)
- [James - The Secret Garden](https://docs.google.com/document/d/JAMES_SECRET_GARDEN_GOOGLE_DOC_ID/edit)

Write under the project report heading in your book packet.
:::
